name: Asthma Response to Inhaled Steroids - Diagnosis Code Query
description: Tests ability to find pediatric/young-adult asthma patients (the cohort for ICS-response PGx). Per PheKB Marshfield algorithm: age 4-35 + asthma dx with acute exacerbation.
prompt: Find young patients (age 4-35) with a diagnosis of asthma with acute exacerbation.

prompts.broad: Find pediatric and young adult patients (age 4-35) diagnosed with asthma with acute exacerbation — the cohort for inhaled corticosteroid response pharmacogenetic studies.
prompts.expert: I need to identify the pediatric and young-adult asthma cohort eligible for the inhaled corticosteroid pharmacogenomics study as defined by the PheKB Marshfield algorithm. Search Condition resources for asthma with acute exacerbation — the ICD-10-CM code for unspecified asthma with acute exacerbation is the target — and restrict the population to patients aged four through thirty-five years, since the ICS-response phenotype is defined in that age window. Do not include stable asthma without exacerbation, as the PheKB algorithm requires documented exacerbation as evidence of active, clinically significant disease.

metadata.algorithm_path_description: Asthma with exacerbation in pediatric/young-adult cohort (PheKB Marshfield ICS PGx).
